source_section.title_label_raw: Built Form and Siting Requirements within the COR Zone Applicability

sections.title_label_raw: Siting Requirements within the COR Zone Applicability

provisions.heading_context_raw: Siting Requirements within the COR Zone Applicability
provisions.text: With the exception of main buildings within a heritage conservation district, any main building erected, constructed, reconstructed, altered, or located, or an addition to any main building, within a COR zone shall meet the built form and siting requirements of this Chapter. Maximum Building Height

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall
provisions.text: Subject to Sections 103 and 104, and Part X, any main building shall not exceed the maximum required building height specified on Schedule 15. Minimum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 178(2), any portion of a main building,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 178(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 178(2), any portion of a main building, either above or below grade, shall have a minimum required front or flanking setback as specified on Schedule 18. (RCCC- Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 178(2), any portion of a main building,
provisions.text: If a minimum required front or flanking setback has not been specified on Schedule 18, the minimum required front or flanking setback shall be 1.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22) Maximum Front or Flanking Setbacks

sections.title_label_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements for any main building located:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property;

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a lot containing a community recreation use; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: on a lot with no maximum front or flanking setbacks identified on Schedule 19.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Subject to Section 180, any main building located along a pedestrian-oriented commercial street, as shown on Schedule 7, shall have a maximum front or flanking setback, as specified on Schedule 19, unless Subsections 179(3), 179(4), or 179(5) apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Maximum front or flanking setbacks shall not apply to portions of lots that have a frontage less than 4.0 metres along a streetline or transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: Where a transportation or utility easement abuts the streetline, a main building shall have a maximum front or flanking setback that is the greater of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the applicable distance as specified on Schedule 19; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: the edge of the easement that is located farthest from the streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: There are no maximum front or flanking setback requirements
provisions.text: The maximum front or flanking setback requirement of Subsection 179(2) may be exceeded, if an at-grade open space, such as a plaza, is located between a front or flanking lot line and a main building. Maximum Front or Flanking Setback Exemption

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 179(3), 179(4), 179(5), and 180(2), no
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 179(3), 179(4), 179(5), and 180(2), no more than 30% of the width of the streetwall may exceed the maximum front or flanking setback requirement of Section 179.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 179(3), 179(4), 179(5), and 180(2), no
provisions.text: Where a development makes use of both Subsections 180(1) and 186(1), the combined streetwall exemptions shall not exceed 30% of the entire width of the streetwall. Side Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum required side setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: subject to Clauses 181(1)(b) and 181(1)(c), where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER- 2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 6.0 metres from the side lot line abutting the lot; (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: for a townhouse dwelling use:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 0.0 metre along a common wall between each unit, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 3.0 metres elsewhere;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: for a semi-detached dwelling use or duplex apartment use: (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 0.0 metre along a common wall between each unit, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 3.0 metres elsewhere; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: 0.0 metre elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum side setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any side yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 181(2) and Section 427, the minimum
provisions.text: There is no maximum required side setback. Rear Setback Requirements

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum required rear setback for any main building shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum
provisions.text: where a lot line abuts a lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, 6.0 metres from the rear lot line abutting the lot; or (RCCC- Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum
provisions.text: 3.0 metres elsewhere.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum
provisions.text: Underground parking structures are not required to have a minimum rear setback, providing they do not protrude more than 0.6 metre above the average finished grade in any rear yard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 182(2) and 182(3), the minimum
provisions.text: For a tall mid-rise building, any portion of the main building exceeding a height of 7 storeys shall have a minimum required setback of 4.5 metres from a rear lot line. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24) Maximum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall be: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: on a registered heritage property, the streetwall height of the registered heritage building on the coming into force date of this By-law;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: for any main building in the Agricola Street (AS) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3F, 2 storeys; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: for a main building except under Clauses 183(1)(a) and 183(1)(b), that is no taller than 5 storeys, the building height; or (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: 3 storeys elsewhere. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Any main building's maximum required streetwall height shall
provisions.text: Repealed (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Minimum Streetwall Heights

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's minimum required streetwall height shall be:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along pedestrian-oriented commercial streets, as shown on Schedule 7, 2 storeys; or (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: along all other streets or transportation reserves:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: for any main building that is 2 storeys or less, the building height, or (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: 2 storeys elsewhere. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: Twenty percent or less of the entire streetwall width, to a maximum of 10.0 metres, may be reduced in height, providing the height is not reduced below 1 storey (Diagram 7). (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 184(2) and 184(3), any main building's
provisions.text: The minimum streetwall height required in Subsection 184(1) may be reduced to 1 storey along a streetline or a transportation reserve, if the slope exceeds 4% across the building width or building depth. (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24) Streetwall Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building shall have a minimum required streetwall stepback of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: 2.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: 3.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for any main building that meets Clause 183(1)(c).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: No streetwall stepback is required for a maximum of 20% of the length of any main building facing each streetline or a transportation reserve provided that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: each section not having a streetwall stepback does not exceed 14.0 metres in length along the streetline or transportation reserve; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: there is a minimum required separation distance of 14.0 metres along the streetline or transportation reserve between sections not having a streetwall stepback. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 185(2) and 185(3), any main building
provisions.text: No portion of any main building above the streetwall shall project beyond the streetwall towards the front or flanking lot line. Recessed Portions and Cantilevers within Streetwalls

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsections 186(2) and 186(3), the total combined

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 186(2) and 186(3), the total combined
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 186(2) and 186(3), the total combined width of all cantilevered and recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2.0 metres or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 186(2) and 186(3), the total combined
provisions.text: Cantilevered and recessed portions described in Subsection 186(1) shall include portals, but exclude balconies and recessed pedestrian entrances.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 186(2) and 186(3), the total combined
provisions.text: Where a development makes use of both Subsections 186(1) and 180(1), the combined streetwall exemptions shall not exceed 30% of the entire width of the streetwall. Maximum Lot Coverage

sections.title_label_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.

provisions.heading_context_raw: No maximum required lot coverage applies.
provisions.text: No maximum required lot coverage applies. Ground Floor Requirements

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, a ground floor shall be established along any streetline or transportation reserve. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: On a corner lot, for any streetwall along a streetline or transportation reserve that has an average finished grade that exceeds a slope greater than 8%, extended across the entire width of the streetwall, the requirements for a ground floor do not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: A ground floor shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline or a transportation reserve.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 188(6), each ground floor required shall, for no less than 70% of each streetwall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 0.6 metre below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 188(5), commence no higher than 0.6 metre above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: In the event of a conflict between the coastal elevation requirements of Section 74 and Clause 188(4)(b), Clause 188(4)(b) shall not apply.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor of a grade-related dwelling unit use shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: subject to Section 74, commence no lower than 1.5 metres below the streetline grade; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: commence no higher than 1.5 metres above the streetline grade.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 188(8) and excluding a low-density dwelling use, a grade-related dwelling unit use, or a residential lobby on a floor containing a grade-related dwelling unit use, the minimum required ground floor height shall be 3.5 metres. (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: The ground floor height required in Subsection 188(7) may be reduced where one of the following conditions applies:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: the ground floor height of an addition is equal to or greater than the ground floor height of the existing main building; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 188(2) and excluding 100-Series
provisions.text: for a proposed building on a registered heritage property, the ground floor heights of abutting main buildings along the same streetline are such that the required ground floor height of the proposed main building would be inconsistent with the abutting main buildings. Grade-Oriented Premises

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented premises shall:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: excluding 100-Series Highways, as shown on Schedule 52, be located along any streetline; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E- Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: be required for the full width of the streetwall, excluding:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: only one entrance to internal parking located above or below the ground floor, (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a portal,

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency exit, or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an entrance to a room dedicated to a solid waste management area; (RCCC-Oct 26/22;E-Nov 11/22)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: not exceed a maximum width of 24.0 metres for each premise facing a streetline: and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: contain an independent pedestrian entrance for each premise facing a streetline.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Subsection 189(1) shall not apply to:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a change of use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a low-density dwelling use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a religious institution use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: an emergency services use;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a cultural use; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: a shared housing with special care use. (RCCC-Jan 24/24;E-Feb 9/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: For any addition to any main building, the requirement of Subsection 189(1) shall only apply to the length of the streetwall addition.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsections 189(2) and 189(3), grade-oriented
provisions.text: Grade-oriented premises shall have a minimum required depth of 3.0 metres, as measured from any exterior wall of any main building that is facing a streetline. Grade-Related Dwelling Units

sections.title_label_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of

provisions.heading_context_raw: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of
provisions.text: Every grade-related dwelling unit shall have a required minimum of one exterior pedestrian entrance. Side and Rear Stepbacks

sections.title_label_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any portion of which, is zoned ER-3, ER-2, ER-1, CH-2, CH-1, PCF, or RPK zone, the wall of any main building facing the lot with such zone shall have a required side or rear stepback, as measured from the lowest finished grade on that side of the main building, on the 2nd, 3rd, or 4th storey. (RC- May 23/24;E- June 13/24) (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: The side or rear stepback in Subsection 191(1) shall be a minimum of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 0.0 metre for low-rise buildings;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 2.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 6.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 6.0 metres for high-rise buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: The side or rear stepback under Subsection 191(1) is not required if an entire main building has a minimum side or rear setback of: (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 8.0 metres for mid-rise buildings; (RC- May 23/24;E-June 13/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 12.0 metres for tall mid-rise buildings; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Subject to Subsection 191(3), where a lot abuts another lot, any
provisions.text: 12.0 metres for high-rise buildings. Minimum Separation Distances

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main building is permitted on a lot in Subsection 89(3), a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between main buildings.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Where more than one portion of any main building protrudes above grade, a minimum required separation distance of 4.0 metres shall be provided between the above grade portions.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: Above the streetwall height, any portions of the same main building shall be separated by a minimum required distance of:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 6.0 metres between a mid-rise typology and another mid- rise typology;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 6.0 metres between a mid-rise typology and a tall mid-rise typology; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding pedways or tunnels, where more than one main
provisions.text: 12.5 metres between a tall mid-rise typology and another tall mid-rise typology. (RC-Jul 12/22;E-Aug 22/22) Maximum Building Dimensions

sections.title_label_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average finished grade or any public building use, any main building shall not exceed:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building width of 64.0 metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: a building depth of 64.0 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Excluding any structure below 0.6 metre above the average
provisions.text: For the purpose of measuring building dimensions in Subsection 193(1), main buildings connected by a pedway shall be measured separately, excluding the pedway. Accessory Structures

sections.title_label_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter

provisions.heading_context_raw: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter
provisions.text: Accessory structures shall meet the requirements of Part V, Chapter 19.
